_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Confer with systems analysts, engineers, programmers, and others to design application and to obtain information on project limitations and capabilities, performance requirements, and interfaces.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing technical documentation for data systems, including architecture diagrams, API specifications, operational runbooks, data dictionaries, and troubleshooting guides that enable team independence.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-S-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-001 (TF, cosine: 0.539), AB-K-006 (AB, cosine: 0.493).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.6/10 (raw 0.464). Shared vocabulary: [systems]. DCWF-only terms [analysts, application, capabilities, confer, design, engineers] vs WIDAI-only terms [api, architecture, data, diagrams, dictionaries, documentation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.870, R=0.864). Balanced P/R — the 24-word DCWF element and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.3%). "Confer with systems analysts, engineers, programmers, and others to design appli" and "Skill in writing technical documentation for data systems, including architectur" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.585 > STS 0.464 (gap +0.121). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [systems] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.9%. Shared tokens: [systems]. DCWF-only: [analysts, application, capabilities, confer, design, engineers]. WIDAI-only: [api, architecture, data, diagrams, dictionaries, documentation].

signals.detail: STS 0.464 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.121 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
